extracted_items.content: Evidence index persists across `/new` command — SQLite connection closes but file stays on disk, allowing new sessions to search previous conversation tool history
Phase 2 of memory improvements: Extraction Decoupling — decouple extraction from compaction to run on independent schedule (~20 user turns, /new, idle flush, catch-up before compaction)
Extraction watermark tracking — maintain `last_extracted_index` in session metadata to prevent silent memory loss from failed extraction batches and ensure catch-up before compaction
Smarter extraction routing — categorize extracted items as shared (all agents visible in memory graph) vs agent-specific (scoped in memory graph), stops dumping shared facts into MEMORY.md
Phase 3: MEMORY.md Compaction — after extraction, check MEMORY.md token count; if over 10K tokens, use Haiku to distill to ~5K, keeping only agent-private preferences and active context

Extraction window with context overlap — provide Haiku with read-only context messages before watermark (5 messages) plus extraction window (new messages since last extraction), explicitly marked so Haiku knows not to extract from context
Extraction token budget — cap input at ~12K tokens per batch to keep Haiku focused; batch large windows into multiple smaller extractions if needed

Graph-aware extraction with dedup — include top relevant existing memories in extraction prompt so Haiku can make informed decisions on adding new items, superseding outdated ones, or reinforcing existing memories
Extraction dedup actions — Haiku outputs action field with values: 'add' for new facts, 'reinforce' for existing memories still valid, 'supersede' for newer info contradicting old memories
Watermark advancement guardrail — only advance `last_extracted_index` after ALL items from batch successfully save to graph; if any part fails, watermark stays put for retry at next trigger

Plan: Create isolated test/experiment script using Haiku API to simulate full extraction pipeline before implementation — test last 25 messages from conversation via JSONL, observe what Haiku extracts as important, compare against memory graph results, run fuzz tests and scenarios to validate system reliability
